name: Preact
description: Fast 3kB alternative to React with the same modern API

description.value: Fast 3kB alternative to React with the same modern API

active-maintenance.comment: Multiple releases in 2025; Preact 11 beta also in development

reactivity-model.comment: Uses Virtual DOM diffing; also offers @preact/signals for fine-grained reactivity

component-model.comment: Supports both function components with hooks and class components, same API as React

rendering-paradigm.comment: Declarative UI rendering via JSX, same paradigm as React

state-management-approach.comment: @preact/signals provides built-in global state; also works with external libraries

opinionatedness.comment: Minimal core library, flexible like React; doesn't prescribe architecture

backward-compatibility.comment: preact/compat provides React compatibility layer; stable v10 API

primary-language.comment: Written in JavaScript (96%) with TypeScript types (4%)

typescript-support.comment: Full TypeScript support with included type definitions; TSX works out of the box

template-syntax.comment: JSX/TSX same as React; also supports HTM for build-less development

styling-approach.comment: No built-in styling; works with CSS modules, CSS-in-JS, Tailwind, etc.

build-required.comment: Can use via CDN with HTM (no JSX build needed); build recommended for production

computed-values.comment: useMemo hook; @preact/signals provides computed() for derived values

two-way-binding.comment: One-way data flow like React; controlled components require onChange handlers

event-handling.comment: Uses browser's native addEventListener, not synthetic events like React

conditional-rendering.comment: Via ternary operators, && short-circuit in JSX, same as React

list-rendering.comment: Via Array.map() in JSX with key prop, same as React

component-slots.comment: children prop for content projection, same as React

suspense.comment: Suspense and lazy() available via preact/compat; limitations in v10, improved in v11

error-boundaries.comment: componentDidCatch and getDerivedStateFromError supported in class components

context-provide-inject.comment: createContext/useContext API for dependency injection, same as React

refs-direct-dom-access.comment: useRef hook and ref prop for direct DOM access

official-router.comment: preact-router is maintained by Preact team; preact-iso provides modern alternative

router-name.comment: preact-router is official; preact-iso is newer with better nested route support

nested-routes.comment: Basic support in preact-router; better support in preact-iso or via react-router with compat

route-guards.comment: Via router's match module and programmatic navigation

lazy-loading-routes.comment: Via lazy() and dynamic imports, or AsyncRoute module

scroll-restoration.comment: Not built-in; can be implemented manually or via react-router with compat

built-in-state.comment: useState, useReducer hooks for local component state

global-state-library.comment: Official signals library; also works with Zustand, Redux via compat

devtools-state-inspection.comment: Preact DevTools browser extension supports state inspection

state-persistence.comment: No built-in persistence; available via external libraries

time-travel-debugging.comment: Not built-in; possible via Redux DevTools with redux integration

official-cli.comment: create-preact is the official CLI; preact-cli exists but deprecated in favor of Vite

browser-devtools.comment: Preact DevTools extension for Chrome, Firefox, and Edge

vs-code-extension.comment: Works with React/JSX extensions; no Preact-specific extension needed

error-messages.comment: Helpful error messages; preact/debug adds development warnings

documentation-quality.comment: Good documentation with guides, API reference, and interactive tutorial

bundle-size-core.comment: ~4 kB gzipped for core preact package

bundle-size-full.comment: ~6.8 kB gzipped for preact + hooks (v10.25.4); preact/compat adds ~2kB more

virtual-dom.comment: Uses Virtual DOM with optimized diffing algorithm

compile-time-optimization.comment: Runtime-based; no compile-time optimizations like Svelte

tree-shaking.comment: ES modules support tree shaking via bundlers

lazy-components.comment: lazy() via preact/compat for lazy loading components

ssr-capable.comment: preact-render-to-string for SSR with hydration support

streaming-ssr.comment: renderToPipeableStream and renderToReadableStream for streaming SSR

partial-hydration.comment: Progressive hydration via preact-iso islands; improved in Preact 11

js-framework-benchmark.comment: Benchmark scores vary; Preact performs well in virtual DOM category

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: Approximately 11.3 million weekly downloads as of December 2025

component-libraries.comment: preact-material-components, preact-mdl, @material-design/preact, Preact Fluid; also works with React libraries via compat

meta-framework.comment: Fresh (Deno) uses Preact; Vike supports Preact; can use with Astro

major-companies-using.value: Uber, Groupon, Lyft, HashiCorp, DEV, daily.dev

official-sponsor-backer.comment: Community-driven project; created by Jason Miller (now at Google)

discord-community-size.comment: No official Discord; Preact community uses Slack (chat.preactjs.com)

stack-overflow-questions.comment: Questions tagged 'preact' on Stack Overflow; smaller than React community

job-market-demand.comment: Niche but growing; often listed alongside React skills; used for performance-critical apps

official-testing-library.comment: @testing-library/preact is community standard from Testing Library org

testing-library-name.comment: Official Testing Library support for Preact; works with Jest or Vitest

snapshot-testing.comment: Via Jest or Vitest snapshot testing

mobile-framework.comment: No official mobile framework; can use with Capacitor or Tauri

pwa-support.comment: PWA support built into create-preact and preact-cli scaffolding

desktop-framework.comment: No official desktop framework; works with Tauri, Electron (framework-agnostic)

learning-curve.comment: Easy for React developers; same API means minimal new concepts to learn

concepts-to-learn.comment: Same concepts as React: JSX, Components, Props, State, Hooks, Virtual DOM; plus preact/compat if using React libs

time-to-hello-world.comment: ~3 minutes with create-preact; can be instant with CDN + HTM (no build)

migration-path.comment: preact/compat provides near 100% React compatibility for easy migration

incremental-adoption.comment: Can be added to existing apps; drop-in replacement for React with aliasing
